title: A cascade hole-transfer strategy towards stable hybrid perovskite solar cells - Nature Energy
gist: A cascade hole-transfer strategy using a deep-level donor polymer simultaneously suppresses charge recombination and improves intrinsic moisture stability in hybrid perovskite/organic solar cells, addressing both efficiency and durability — the two central barriers to commercialization of perovskite photovoltaics.

themes.theme: Perovskite Solar Cell Stability and Commercialization

insights.claim: Perovskite solar cells are considered a leading candidate for next-generation commercial photovoltaics but remain commercially constrained by sensitivity to heat and moisture that limits practical deployment.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: Heat and moisture instability remains the primary technical risk factor for investors in perovskite PV companies; solutions addressing both simultaneously are high-value IP.
insights.quote: Perovskite solar cells are a leading candidate for next-generation commercial photovoltaics, but their sensitivity to heat and moisture limits their practical use.

insights.claim: A cascade hole-transfer strategy using a deep-level donor polymer in hybrid perovskite/organic solar cells simultaneously suppresses charge recombination and provides intrinsic moisture stability — without requiring encapsulation.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: Unencapsulated moisture stability eliminates a major manufacturing cost and complexity barrier, improving the commercial viability calculus for perovskite PV producers.
insights.quote: a cascade hole-transfer strategy is developed to suppress charge recombination in hybrid perovskite/organic solar cells while offering intrinsic moisture stability

insights.claim: The sub-nanometer dipole interface approach in hybrid perovskite/organic solar cells enhances near-infrared photon harvesting by suppressing hole accumulation at the perovskite-organic interface, expanding the usable solar spectrum.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: Near-infrared harvesting improvements in tandem architectures expand total energy yield per installed watt, strengthening the economics of perovskite-based PV relative to silicon.
insights.quote: HSCs can enhance near-infrared harvesting, while highlighting the importance of electronic-structure control to suppress hole accumulation at the perovskite–organic interface
